rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Aerojet-General Corporation To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition, Pursuant To The July 5, 2011, Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Aerojet-General Corp. to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition, pursuant to the July 5, 2011 subpoena is continued on the court¿s own motion to September 21, 2011 at 9:00am to give the parties additional time to meet and confer. If all issues raised by this motion have not been resolved before then, by 4:30pm on September 20 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on September 21. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT SETNIK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting Plaintiff From: 1) Taking The Deposition Of Rcas Person Most Knowledgeable, And 2) Requiring Production Of Documents By Rcas Custodian Of Records As Designated In Plaintiffs Deposition Notice; Or Alternatively 3) Limiting Scope Of Deposition Notice
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant RCA Corporation¿s motion for protective order prohibiting plaintiff from taking the deposition of its person most knowledgeable and requiring production of documents is denied and its alternate motion for protective order limiting the scope of the deposition is granted. The deposition is limited to the dates listed on Exhibit A of plaintiff¿s deposition notice and to televisions and radios. ¿Asbestos-containing products¿ is not a separate category. Defendant must produce a deponent and documents by October 7, 2011. If the deponent lacks knowledge as to some or all areas after making a reasonable effort to obtain such knowledge, the deponent can so state. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. Defendant¿s position did not lack substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

Defendant¿s position did not lack substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Its Request For Admissions; Verifications To All Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant McCord Corporation¿s motion to compel further responses to its request for admissions is granted. Plaintiffs¿ responses are not code-compliant. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. The court assumes that plaintiffs¿ responses are the result of a good faith disagreement with the court as to what is required by the RFA rules and thus will not impose sanction this time. Also some of the points raised at the informal conference did not lack substantial justification. = (220/HEK)
